close_reason: Implemented in b6b6f65a1: activation-cycle edges are derived from live CallSiteSummary fact changes, maintained incrementally per callsite, and classified with one SCC pass; remaining Enum.sort timeout is tracked by fz-rh2.18.9.12.
description:
Goal
Make activation-cycle maintenance proportional to changed root callsite edges, not a full root graph rebuild plus recursive activation-key canonicalization on every changed callsite summary. The current implementation can spend the entire run in `AnalyzeActivation -> define_callsite_summary -> refresh_activation_cycles -> activation_key -> canonical_activation_key` on `fixtures2/00285_enum_sort_stable.fz`; clean HEAD and the .9.10 worktree both time out under isolated `cargo test` runs.

Signal
- `refresh_activation_cycles` no longer rebuilds/canonicalizes every root edge on each changed callsite publication. Callsite publication owns durable root-scoped activation edges and replaces only the changed callsite's edge set.
- Cycle classification consumes the durable edge graph with one SCC pass, not per-edge reachability over the whole root graph.
- A focused telemetry run for `fixtures2/00285_enum_sort_stable.fz` shows the previous `refresh_activation_cycles -> activation_key -> canonical_activation_key` hotspot is gone; any remaining timeout is filed as `fz-rh2.18.9.12` and is caused by semantic return-readiness churn, not cycle refresh.
- Existing dynamic-cycle correctness tests still prove cyclic activations ascend while acyclic activations can replace stale evidence.
- The new edge replacement test proves removing one callsite edge does not erase a duplicate edge from another callsite.

Strategy
- Start with the existing failing shape: reproduce the sort test timeout and keep the stack-sample evidence in mind, not as a permanent artifact.
- Move cycle maintenance data-model-up: callsite publication should update durable root-scoped edges, and cycle classification should consume those edges without re-canonicalizing all prior summaries.
- Preserve the semantic model from .9.7: cycles are activation-dataflow facts, not source recursive-function guesses.
- When the cycle-refresh hotspot is removed, split newly exposed semantic convergence failures into their own tickets instead of broadening this commit into mixed concerns.
- Do not hide the problem with longer timeouts, disabled tests, Enum special cases, or artifact-demand-only workarounds.

Outro
No whole-root cycle graph rebuild per callsite, no repeated canonicalization of old edges, no naive reachability loop per edge, no timeout increase, no ignored sort/wider-suite test, no static Recursive(function) fallback, and no Enum-specific bypass.
title: Compiler2 activation cycles: make cycle refresh incremental and bounded
